title: The House of Obedience
subtitle: Mary breaks the alabaster and pours costly nard over the head of Christ at Bethany

stillness: Be still, and draw near to the house of obedience.
Bethany, the place where Lazarus had died and was raised.
Let your soul grow quiet, and follow the Lord as he comes to his offering.
gospel: Six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany.
He entered the house of Simon, once a leper, now made clean.
There they made him a supper.
And Lazarus was one of those reclining at table with him.
Then Mary came, and drew near to Jesus.
She had an alabaster jar of precious ointment of pure nard.
And, the alabaster broken, she poured the ointment upon his head as he reclined.
And she anointed also the feet of Jesus,
and wiped his feet with her hair.
behold: As the time drew near in which the Lord had resolved to suffer, he drew near to the place where he willed to accomplish his Passion;
by this he made known that he would suffer willingly, of his own accord, and undergo death.
Knowing that the Jews had conspired in his death, as the Passover drew near, in which the paschal lamb was slain,
he came back as a lamb to the victim, that the true Lamb might be offered for us immediately after the slaying of the paschal lamb.
He came to Bethany, where Lazarus had died, whom he raised, that the raising of Lazarus might be renewed and pressed more deeply into the memory of all.
And he was in the house of Simon, once a leper, then cleansed, who still kept his former name to the memory of the miracle, that the power of the Healer might appear.
From this we may gather that, though we have been cleansed from our sin, we ought always to keep it in memory unto penance.
Mary drew near to Jesus as to a fountain of mercy, washing away our infirmities,
having an alabaster jar full of precious ointment;
and, the alabaster broken, she poured the ointment over his head as he sat at table, as rose-water is poured out for refreshment and fragrance;
and she anointed also his feet, with whom even before she had found such grace, and wiped his feet with her hair.
consider: Believe with devotion that the supper was honorable and without excess, made for him who was the preacher and lover of frugality.
Lazarus was one of those reclining with him, that the true miracle of his raising might be declared.
Lest men should think it a phantom that the dead one rose, Lazarus was one of those at table: he was living, he was speaking, he was feasting.
The truth was shown forth; the unbelief of the Jews was confounded.
He came to Bethany, which is interpreted the house of obedience.
For, according to the Apostle, he was made obedient to the Father unto death.
About to suffer for the whole world, and to redeem all nations by his blood, he comes and abides in Bethany, the house of obedience.
The fawn of the harts always returns to its own bed; and the Son, obedient to the Father unto death, asks obedience of us.
In the house of obedience, those who are spiritually dead in their sins are raised again and led back to the life of justice.
The Lord, in the house of obedience, is spiritually refreshed, delighted in our obedience.
Mary anointed the head and the feet of Jesus, and wiped them with her hair: by her are signified the contemplatives,
for of her it is said elsewhere that, sitting at the Lord's feet, she heard his word.
prayer: who, about to suffer for the whole world, willed to come and abide in Bethany, that is, in the house of obedience,
and there to sup, and there to let your head and your feet be anointed:
grant me, persevering in true obedience, to be worthy to make for you this spiritual refreshment;
for you are delighted in our obedience, and in a manner are fed by it.
Make me also there to anoint your head and your feet:
the head, confessing with worthy reverence and praise the power of your divine virtue;
the feet, worthily receiving the mysteries of your assumed humanity;
or the head, by venerating you, O Christ, and the feet, by serving your faithful ones,
that so acting I may obtain your grace. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Passurus Dominus pro omni mundo, et universas nationes suo sanguine redempturus, venit et moratur in Bethania, id est in domo obedientiæ.
latinJewel.english: The Lord, about to suffer for the whole world, and to redeem all nations by his blood, comes and abides in Bethany, that is, in the house of obedience.
